headline: OpenTable launches Gold Tables + sunsets Visa deal in same week
detail: Gold status (6 bookings/yr, no fee) now unlocks primetime seats at 500+ hard-to-book restaurants in 50+ cities, with Priority Notify Me alerts for last-minute openings — simultaneously, the Visa Dining Collection US program was sunsetted as OpenTable consolidates access entirely inside its own Gold Regulars tier.
so_what: OpenTable is weaponizing loyalty to own the access layer — direct threat to ResX's core value prop, and the Visa exit closes a formerly neutral entry point for diners.

headline: Resy launches Summer Seat with Amex Gold
detail: Art-driven outdoor dining series at Bonnie's NYC, Lasita LA, Mi Tocaya Chicago; Gold Card early access live July 7.
so_what: Resy deepening Amex cardholder lock-in with exclusive access — raises the bar on experiential bookings.

headline: Resy and Amex launch Restaurant Academy program
detail: Free national leadership program for 30 restaurant owners; apps open July 1–29, cohort Sept 2026–Mar 2027.
so_what: Resy embedding deeper into restaurant operator relationships — not just bookings, but business development.

headline: Dorsia expands to Chicago
detail: Dorsia announces Chicago as a new market, confirmed via Instagram Reel.
so_what: another top reservation competitor adds a major US city ResX should track

headline: Supreme Court TPS ruling hits restaurant labor now
detail: 330,000 Haitian/Syrian workers lose authorization; NRA petitions DHS for transition period.
so_what: Staffing gaps at partner restaurants mid-summer could spike no-shows and service disruptions at high-demand venues.

headline: Guest regulars quietly churning at record pace in H1
detail: Only 17 of 100 H2 2025 'regulars' still visiting at same rate; 29% vanished entirely in 2026.
so_what: Direct data point for why retention-layer features — not just acquisition — matter in the reservation product roadmap.

headline: Glen Powell's Smash Kitchen Hits the Met Cart
detail: Free specialty hot dogs outside The Met, July 15 only, 500 total — two limited-edition creations (Hot Honey Dog with Smash Kitchen Hot Honey Ketchup + crushed kettle chips; Caesar Dog with Caesar Dressing) plus a live hot dog calligrapher decorating franks with Smash Kitchen condiments. Joint activation with HexClad.
so_what: Glen Powell brand activating at NYC's most iconic cart on National Hot Dog Day week — celeb-brand meets street culture, peak cultural moment for our audience.

headline: Coqodaq's London Debut Ends Saturday
detail: Caviar nuggets at Lucky Cat Mayfair close July 12 — final days.
so_what: NYC cult restaurant testing London demand via Gordon Ramsay — watch conversion signal.

headline: Rosewood London Terrace Goes Cult Pizzeria
detail: Dinner For One Hundred residency live in Holborn courtyard this summer.
so_what: Hotel using cult south London operator to drive F&B bookings — hotel x local operator playbook.

headline: Myka x Losers cinnamon roll froyo topping
detail: Myka's West Village Greek froyo now tops with mini Losers cinnamon roll bites, a monthly special.
so_what: exactly the collab-of-the-month energy the audience is already discussing

headline: Levain debuts Cookie Milk Latte
detail: Oat milk steeped 12hrs with 3 signature cookies, espresso, salted cold foam — launched July 6, $7.50 nationwide.
so_what: turns Levain's cookies-and-milk pairing into a coffee format for the first time

headline: Anthropic ships Claude Sonnet 5 at intro price
detail: Near-Opus-4.8 quality at $2/$10 per million tokens through Aug 31
so_what: Migrate Node/TypeScript API calls from Opus to Sonnet 5 now — 40–60% cost cut before Sept 1 price step-up

headline: Kimi K2.7 Code lands in GitHub Copilot picker
detail: First open-weight model in Copilot; lower-cost billing, Azure-hosted
so_what: Enable in VS Code to test on routine TypeScript edits and cut Copilot credit spend — hold off on proprietary code until provenance is reviewed
